channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Canberra Community Pushes for Stadium Upgrades Led by Matt Ward
item.description: Canberra locals are actively campaigning for improvements to Canberra Stadium, with NDIS worker Matt Ward spearheading a petition that has nearly reached 3,000 signatures. The movement began after Can...

item.title: Government Issues 48-Hour Deadline to BMA Over Doctor Strike and Training Posts
item.description: The Prime Minister has issued a 48-hour ultimatum to the British Medical Association (BMA), demanding they call off a six-day strike by resident doctors in England, or risk the loss of 1,000 additiona...

item.title: Trump’s Threat to Iran’s Kharg Island and Its Strategic Implications
item.description: Kharg Island, a small patch of land in the Gulf roughly one-third the size of Manhattan, plays a vital role in Iran’s oil exports, handling about 90 per cent of the country’s crude shipments. Recently...

item.title: Experts Discuss Legality of Military Action Against Iran and Advocate Peaceful Solutions
item.description: The news article discusses a debate among legal experts and diplomats regarding the legality of military intervention against Iran. Several advocates and international diplomats came together to analy...

item.title: Kemi Badenoch criticised for rushed decisions on Iran war and energy policy
item.description: The article offers a satirical critique of Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch, portraying her as impulsive and prone to attention-seeking behaviour. It highlights her initial eagerness to support the U...

item.title: Traders Risk Lives by Operating on Oil Pipeline and Power Lines in Dar es Salaam
item.description: In Dar es Salaam, densely populated areas like Mbagala Zakheim, Gongo la Mboto and New Station to Pugu have become busy economic zones where small traders, food vendors, and bodaboda drivers earn thei...

item.title: Steps to Take When Water Contaminates Your Vehicle’s Fuel Tank
item.description: The article provides practical guidance for vehicle owners dealing with water contamination in their fuel tanks, a problem that can lead to serious engine damage and poor performance. It explains that...

item.title: At Least 26 Killed in Palm Sunday Attack on Plateau Community
item.description: Residents of Anguwan Rukuba in Jos North Local Government Area of Plateau State have confirmed that at least 26 people lost their lives in a brutal Palm Sunday night attack. According to a video seen ...

item.title: PMDC investigates video recording incident inside Lahore hospital operating theatre
item.description: Islamabad: The Pakistan Medical and Dental Council (PMDC) has formally taken notice of a troubling incident at Lahore's Lady Willingdon Hospital where a video was filmed during ongoing surgical proced...

item.title: Shah Criticizes Gandhi's Statements on Modi, Advocates for Legal Recourse
item.description: Union Home Minister Amit Shah has strongly condemned the recent remarks made by Congress leader Rahul Gandhi concerning Prime Minister Narendra Modi. Shah characterized Gandhi’s statements as “defamat...

item.title: Reported Similarities in Thoughts About JFK Jr. Spark Discussion
item.description: A recent article has sparked considerable discussion online after claiming many women are experiencing similar, explicit thoughts related to the late John F. Kennedy Jr. The article proposes that a cl...

item.title: Minister Orders NSFAS Forensic Unit to Investigate Fraud and Improve Student Services
item.description: Higher Education and Training Minister Buti Manamela has instructed the National Student Financial Aid Scheme (NSFAS) to immediately deploy its forensic unit to collaborate with the Special Investigat...

item.title: Trump claims role in resolving multiple international conflicts, criticizes Obama
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump don cause yawa again after him talk say na im dey deserve the Nobel Peace Prize pass anybody wey don live for history. For one video wey don dey circulate for internet, Tru...

item.title: Delhi High Court orders strict enforcement against illegal vendors at Nehru Place
item.description: The Delhi High Court has declined to grant any relief to street vendors operating in the busy Nehru Place area, directing the authorities to take strict action against illegal hawking. The court’s dec...

item.title: Cape Town tenders to buy 500MW from private power traders to lower electricity costs
item.description: The City of Cape Town (CoCT) has announced a major initiative to reduce its dependence on Eskom by procuring 500 megawatts of electricity from licensed private energy traders. The move aims to cut ele...

item.title: NTC prepares measures to ensure steady communication services during Holy Week 2026
item.description: The National Telecommunications Commission (NTC) has rolled out nationwide efforts to guarantee stable and continuous communication throughout Holy Week 2026. In a public statement issued on March 30,...

item.title: White House suggests Gulf nations could contribute financially to military campaign
item.description: According to reports from Washington, the White House is considering a plan under which Gulf states may be asked to help finance a potential military campaign led by the United States. Senior official...

item.title: Names of Victims Killed, Kidnapped, and Injured in Southern Kaduna Wedding Attack Released
item.description: For Sunday, di people wey dey Kukyer and Kahir villages, Kagarko LGA, Kaduna State, suffer heavy wahala as bandits attack wedding ceremony. Di attack lead to 13 people wey dem kill, 28 people wey dem ...

item.title: How to Bake Delicious Homemade Hot Cross Buns with Beatrix Bakes' Recipe
item.description: In this Easter-themed feature for The Canberra Times, journalist Karen Hardy invites readers to skip mass-produced supermarket hot cross buns and instead bake their own using a detailed recipe from Na...

item.title: Johannesburg petrol stations limit fuel sales amid supply delays linked to Middle East conflict
item.description: Several petrol stations across Johannesburg have begun rationing fuel, restricting motorists to 30 litres per vehicle as fuel supply delays linked to the ongoing conflict between the US, Israel, and I...
